forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: Partly cloudy, with a low around 42. Southwest wind around 3 mph.

forecast.name: Saturday
forecast.detailedForecast: Rain showers likely between 7am and 5pm, then showers and thunderstorms. Cloudy, with a high near 61. South wind 2 to 10 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.

weather_report:
As the clock ticks into the early hours of Saturday, Boston nestles under a partly cloudy sky, the air nipping gently at 42 degrees. The night retains a hush, broken only by the distant hum of life, as a light southwest breeze meanders through the streets. Shimmering lights punctuate the dark, their reflections dancing on the surface of the Charles River, weaving an ambiance of solitude and promise.

Yet, the dawn brings with it a shift. By day, the sky will be painted with heavy clouds, heralding rain showers that begin early and persist through the afternoon, stirring the air with the earthy scent of impending rain. Temperatures will rise only moderately, peeking at a high of 61, as the winds shift southward, bringing whispers of moisture. With an 80% chance of precipitation, umbrellas will emerge from their seasonal slumber, while those venturing out should consider layers—a light jacket over a comfortable shirt, perhaps, to accommodate the rhythmic warmth of the day intertwined with its cooler, damp moments. 

In this weather, today may be well suited for pursuits that embrace the cozy comforts of home—an afternoon inside a favorite café, cradling a warm beverage while watching the rain streak down the windows, or losing oneself in the pages of a beloved book. For the adventurous spirit, a stroll through Boston’s historic streets may offer a poignant beauty, where raindrops dance on cobblestones and the soft patter against your umbrella adds a soundtrack to the intimate tapestry of city life.
